title: What diversity in education really looks like – and why it matters
description: SCHOOL NEWS - South Africa s classrooms mirror the complexity of our broader society: a blend of cultures, languages, histories, and perspectives, working towards a common goal. And, while this

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SCHOOL NEWS - South Africa’s...
page.bodyText:
Contributor
                                Shaun Fuchs, CEO Centennial Schools

SCHOOL NEWS - South Africa’s classrooms mirror the complexity of our broader society: a blend of cultures, languages, histories, and perspectives, working towards a common goal.

And, while this diversity is one of our greatest strengths, it brings with it the responsibility to actively foster inclusion, equality, and respect.

In a nation still reckoning with our history and the many inequities that persist, our education system holds tremendous power to either reinforce divides or to heal them.

For Shaun Fuchs, CEO and founder of Centennial Schools, it is clear which path we must take.

“We need to reimagine schools as inclusive environments where all learners feel seen, respected and empowered.” And, says Fuchs, diversity is not a ‘nice to have’ – it is a national imperative. “In our country, with 12 official languages and over 50 cultural groups, inclusion should not be treated as a theme for one day a year. It must be embedded in the school curriculum, reflected in leadership, and lived out in culture.”

Research supports this. Studies from UNESCO and the OECD show that inclusive education leads to better academic outcomes, stronger social cohesion, and improved emotional wellbeing.

When students see themselves – their physical identities, languages, traditions, and lived experiences – reflected in the materials they learn from, the peers they learn with, and the people who teach them, they develop a deeper sense of belonging.

“It is not just about who is in the room,” Fuchs explains. “It is about who is being seen and heard.”

But this goes beyond representation in textbooks. It is also about challenging stereotypes, fostering intercultural understanding, and creating safe spaces where difficult conversations can happen.

“Inclusion does not mean pretending that there are no differences – it means engaging with them meaningfully. This requires schools to model respectful dialogue and critical thinking, helping students unpack bias, question assumptions, and learn how to engage across lines of difference.”

It also means rethinking what we teach and how we teach it. The traditional schooling model in South Africa has remained largely unchanged since before democracy, often failing to prepare students for a modern, multicultural world.

Future-forward educators are involving students as stakeholders in their own education. These students are an integral part of shaping a learning environment that reflects the realities of the country they are growing up in.

This is the kind of learning that Centennial Schools is embedding. From inclusive language in lesson materials, to diverse guest speakers and ongoing cultural dialogue, the school is intentional about creating a space where diversity is normalised, not marginalised.

“We are not preparing students to succeed in a vacuum,” Fuchs adds. “We are preparing them to thrive in workplaces, communities and countries where empathy and collaboration matter more than ever.”

Ultimately, inclusion is about identity. It is about giving young people the tools to understand who they are and to respect who others are. When schools get this right, they do not just produce better students. They help build a more unified, understanding South Africa.

And Fuchs believes that this is the real goal of education: not just to pass exams, but to build the kind of society that both supports and grows from cultural diversity.

title: Hungry Minds: Why education begins with a full stomach
description: SCHOOL NEWS - We often ask: Why are our schools failing? But perhaps it s time to ask a better question: What do our schools need to succeed? In the global pursuit of educational reform, we tend

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SCHOOL NEWS - We often ask: Why are our...
page.bodyText:
Contributor
                                Nadia Lubowski, Anton Lubowski Educational Trust

SCHOOL NEWS - We often ask: Why are our schools failing? But perhaps it’s time to ask a better question: What do our schools need to succeed?

In the global pursuit of educational reform, we tend to focus on curriculum, infrastructure, or testing outcomes.

Rarely do we pause to consider something far more basic - whether a child has eaten that day. And yet, no syllabus, however well-crafted, can compensate for a child trying to concentrate on an empty stomach.

Hunger is a silent saboteur in the classroom. It dulls concentration, lowers motivation, and impairs memory.

Conversely, when children receive consistent, nutritious meals, they not only perform better academically, but they are more emotionally regulated, socially engaged, and cognitively resilient.

Emerging science reveals something even more profound, the link between gut health and brain function. The gut, often called the "second brain," hosts trillions of bacteria that produce neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, which are key chemicals that affect mood, focus, and memory.

When a child has a healthy gut microbiome, they are biologically better equipped to absorb information, retain it, and engage meaningfully with their environment.

We want to be clear, when we speak about nutrition, what we are talking about and what we provide these children with are nutritionally dense, and healthy whole foods. A varied and complete meal, including whole foods.

In short, food is not separate from education. It is the foundation of it. But nutrition is just one side of the educational coin. The other? Teachers. We must begin to reverence teachers, not just respect them.

They are not merely professionals following a curriculum, they are guardians of our collective future. Each lesson they teach, each moment they invest, ripples across generations.

A teacher can inspire and empower a child to achieve so much more than they ever thought possible.

And yet, teachers are often overworked, underpaid, and overlooked. They are asked to be educators, caregivers, counsellors, and administrators, frequently without adequate support or recognition.

When we fail to invest in their personal well-being, their pedagogical tools, and their professional growth, we do more than risk staff turnover. We risk a systemic collapse, which some may argue, we are currently seeing. We jeopardise the potential of the very children we claim to prioritise.

Imagine if we viewed education as reciprocal, a partnership between student and teacher, family and community, environment and earth.

The classroom, policy and practice thereof holding space for deep learning, to shape the learning experience. What if feeding a child was seen not as charity, but as strategy? What if teacher development wasn’t a line item in the budget, but the very foundation of it?

At the Anton Lubowski Educational Trust, we believe these are not radical questions - they are urgent ones.

The path forward is clear; we must nourish both the body and the mind. We must honour and empower teachers. And we must stop looking for quick fixes and start building lasting foundations.

Because when we invest in children and in those who teach them, we don’t just build schools - we build futures. When we invest in children and those who teach and care for them, we don’t just build schools, we build futures. Active citizens that care about themselves, others and the earth

Banking details for food donations:Anton Lubowski Educational TrustNedbankAcc No: 1010133608Branch Code: 101009Account Type: Current AccountSWIFT Code: NEDSZAJJ

The Anton Lubowski Educational Trust (ALET) was established in 2009 in memory of Namibian anti-apartheid and human rights activist and advocate Anton Lubowski.

Part of the leadership of the South West Africa People’s Organisation (SWAPO), Lubowski was assassinated in September 1989, just three months before Namibia’s first independent elections.

Lubowski felt very strongly that education is the key to a better future and that education had the ability to unite people, irrespective of their race, ethnicity, gender, or creed. In his last interview given before he died, he insisted that education was the most important task of the new Namibian government.

The brainchild of Lubowski’s family, including his daughter Nadia Lubowski and widow Gabrielle Lubowski, ALET focuses on early childhood development in low socio-economic communities, paying special attention to the connection between school and home and helping to set children up for future success.

ALET’s flagship project is an early childhood development and family centre in the Cape Town township of Philippi, an area that suffers high levels of crime, violence, unemployment, and poverty and where children struggle to access good quality education.

ALET’s vision is to help every child coming through its programme reach their full potential and live a purposeful life, breaking the cycle of poverty and hopelessness. Read more about ALET and Anton Lubowski’s legacy.

title: Proteas go down to India in worthwhile T20 World Cup warm up
description: CRICKET NEWS - The Proteas went down to India by 30 runs at the DY Patil Stadium in Navi Mumbai on Wednesday, in their only warm up match ahead of the 2026 T20 World Cup being hosted by India

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: CRICKET NEWS - The Proteas went down to...
page.bodyText:
CRICKET NEWS - The Proteas went down to India by 30 runs at the DY Patil Stadium in Navi Mumbai on Wednesday, in their only warm up match ahead of the 2026 T20 World Cup being hosted by India and Sri Lanka over the next month.

Despite the defeat it was a worthwhile outing for the South Africans, as a number of players got some valuable game time, although they will be a bit disappointed that they weren’t able to take more wickets or put up a better fight.

India won the toss and batted first, slamming their way to a monster 240/5 at the end of their 20 overs, with Ishan Kishan, 53 off 20 balls (2×4; 7×6), and Tilak Varma, 45 off 19 (3×4; 3×6), at the top of the order, and Hardik Pandya, 30 off 10 (2×4; 3×6) at the end making big contributions.

But every batter pitched in as Axar Patel, 35no off 23 (2×4; 2×6), Suryakumar Yadav, 30 off 16 (2×4; 2×6), Abhishek Sharma, 24 off 18 (3×4; 1×6), and Rinku Singh, 16 off 13 (1×4; 1×6) also contributed.

The Proteas bowlers largely struggled, as only four of the eight used picked up a wicket each, namely Marco Jansen, Kwena Maphaka, Corbin Bosch and the very expensive Anrich Nortje, while India retired out Kishan and Sharma retired hurt.

The Proteas chase was then lit up by five players, but they finished well short of their target in the end, however it was good to see David Miller successfully return from an injury picked up in the SA20, although he only scored 13.

Captain Aiden Markram slammed 38 off 19 balls (2×4; 4×6) before retiring out, Ryan Rickelton clubbed 44 off 21 (4×4; 3×6), and Jason Smith got in some valuable time in the middle hitting 35 off 23 (4×4; 1×6).

At the end of the innings Tristan Stubbs had a bit of fun, crashing three of the last four balls of the match for six in his 45no off 21 (1×4; 4×6), while Jansen knocked 31 off 16 (3×4; 1×6), as they finished on 210/7.

The Proteas open their T20 World Cup campaign against Canada in Ahmedabad on Monday night, before playing Afghanistan, New Zealand and the United Arab Emirates in their other pool matches.

title: Groms to take to the waves at Sea Harvest Grommet Games
description: SURFING NEWS - Twenty-three young surfers, also known as grommets or groms, from Eden have been selected to the Eden Surf Riders team for 2026. They will represent the Garden Route in the Sea

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SURFING NEWS - Twenty-three young surfers,...
page.bodyText:
SURFING NEWS - Twenty-three young surfers, also known as grommets or groms, from Eden have been selected to the Eden Surf Riders team for 2026.

They will represent the Garden Route in the Sea Harvest Grommet Games to be held in Muizenberg, Cape Town, on 14 and 15 March.

They hail from Still Bay, Mossel Bay, Victoria Bay, Buffalo Bay and Plettenberg Bay.

This year, more than 50 participants took part in the trials held in Plettenberg Bay and Mossel Bay.

"This junior surfing event is one of South Africa's leading provincial competitions for young surfers and brings together top grommets from across the country for a weekend of high-level competition and development," said Derrick de Necker of Eden Surf Riders.

The event is an important platform for athlete development and competitive exposure, and is part of the pathway to higher-level competitive surfing.

It is supported by Surfing SA, Sea Harvest and the Department of Sports, Arts and Culture.

Young surfers aged U8 to U16 will participate in the games, and while surfing is at the heart of the competition, the games also include paddle battles, beach relays and team-based activities that build camaraderie and confidence.

"The Grommet Games create a safe, structured and inspiring environment for young surfers to experience competitive surfing while learning important life skills," said De Necker.

A new ruling introduced by Surfing SA in 2025 allows former competitors to participate in the Grommet Games again, provided that they did not make the finals of their specific age division in the previous year.

This ruling has been implemented to support development and give more young surfers continued opportunity for participation and growth.

U14 boys: Oliver Bean, Oliver Arvanitakis, Mickayle Loggenberg

title: Evergreens mik weer hoog
description: RUGBY NUUS - As die verdedigende SWD Super A- ligakampioen is Evergreens se doelwit vir die nuwe seisoen glashelder: om weer die beker omhoog te hou en terselfdertyd te wys dat die Green Machine

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NUUS - As die verdedigende SWD Super...
page.bodyText:
Contributor
                                Hugo Redelinghuys

RUGBY NUUS - As die verdedigende SWD Super A- ligakampioen is Evergreens se doelwit vir die nuwe seisoen glashelder: om weer die beker omhoog te hou en terselfdertyd te wys dat die Green Machine ook buite die plaaslike verhoog kan presteer.

Só het Deon Stoffels, voorsitter van Evergreens, die klub se ambisie en voorbereiding vir die seisoen wat voorlê opgesom.

"Ons wil die titel suksesvol verdedig, maar ons is deeglik bewus daarvan dat ons nou in die visier is. Die ander spanne gaan sterker en deegliker voorberei - en dit beteken ons sal ons sokkies moet optrek," erken hy.

Volgens Stoffels is strawwer kompetisie egter geen slegte ding nie. "Ek verwelkom dit. Op die langtermyn gaan dit die standaard van SWD-rugby net verder lig. In die Super A-liga is daar eenvoudig nie klein of groot wedstryde nie - elke week is 'n toets."

Hy verwag veral sterk teenstand van Heidelberg, wat volgens hom oor die afgelope drie seisoene net so maklik kampioen kon gewees het, asook Groot-Brakrivier, Progress, Barbarians en Bridgton, wat almal as gedugte teenstanders beskou word.

'n Verdere belangrike mikpunt vir Evergreens is om reeds in die eerste ronde van die Superliga vir die Goue Beker-kompetisie te kwalifiseer. "Dit beteken ons sal die voortou moet neem en konsekwent wen. Ons wil onsself vroeg reeds in 'n sterk posisie plaas," verduidelik Stoffels.

Op die groter verhoog wil Evergreens ook sy merk laat. "Ons wil baie graag by die Saldanha Paasfees se Top-8-toernooi suksesvol meeding en op die laaste dag in die hoofwedstryd uitdraf. Dit is 'n ideale platform om te wys dat Evergreens meer as net 'n plaaslike krag is."

Wat voorbereiding betref, sê Stoffels dinge is op dreef, maar daar is nog werk om te doen.

"In die volgende twee weke sal ons ons kondisionering met sowat 10% tot 15% moet opskerp. Tegniese aspekte van ons spel geniet ook nou groot aandag."

Hy erken dat oefeninge in Desember swak bygewoon is weens werksverpligtinge, rustyd vir seer lywe en nuwe aanwinste wat na hul onderskeie provinsies teruggekeer het. Sedert Januarie het sake egter drasties verbeter.

"Ons oefen Maandae, Dinsdae en Donderdae en kry gemiddeld tussen 60 en 70 spelers by oefeninge. Wat my werklik bemoedig, is die honger en positiewe gees onder die spelers."

'n Verandering in die afrigtingstruktuur sien Marvin November, oudspeler en hulpafrigter, wat nou die leisels oorneem ná Ricardo Beukes se besluit om sy eie rugby-akademie in Pacaltsdorp te begin.

Evergreens het ook 'n groot groep nuwe spelers verwelkom, insluitend sowat 50 juniorspelers wat hul skoolloopbane voltooi het. "Daar is baie ontluikende talent wat wag vir speelkans," sê Stoffels. "Tog is ons tans effens dun in sleutelposisies - veral op slot, stut en haker."

Die klub het ook verliese gely. Die dinamiese losvoorspeler Leighton van Wyk speel nou klubrugby in Spanje, terwyl Ethan Stuurman na die Rhinos geskuif het.

Twee uitstaande slotte van verlede seisoen, Lububalo 'Giant' Mtyanda en die Zimbabwiër Godwin Mangenje, is ook nog afwesig, hoewel onderhandelinge met hulle aan die gang is.

Stoffels sê een aspek waaraan Evergreens spesifiek aandag gee, is samespel. "Dit het ons verlede seisoen 'n paar keer amper in die steek gelaat, maar ons werk hard daaraan om dit te slyp."

Ten slotte het hy 'n woord van lof vir die klub se ondersteuners. "Ons gemeenskap se ondersteuning is ongelooflik. Die hekinkomste speel 'n reuserol om die rugbywiele aan die rol te hou, en daarvoor is ons opreg dankbaar."

CRICKET NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers Colts will host the Eastern Cape Iinyathi in the Cricket SA provincial Colts competition at the Recreation Ground in Oudtshoorn this weekend.

The three-day match will be played from Thursday to Saturday, while the one-day fixture will be played on Sunday.

In preparation for the CSA One-day Cup that kicks off in two weeks, various professional players will be in action for the Colts, but it will also allow young and upcoming players like Breyton Plaatjies, Kurt Botha, Elih Thorne, Keano Coerecius and Jonathan Bester to show their potential.

page.ogDescription: CRICEKT NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers...
page.bodyText:
CRICEKT NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers women’s team will continue their quest for a place in the final of the Hollywoodbets Pro50 series when they take on the World Sports Betting Western Province at Newlands in Cape Town this weekend.

The Badgers are currently third on the Pro50 log and have an opportunity to play themselves into a place in the finals.

The Oudtshoorn-based team will go without Annerie Dercksen, who is on national duty, and Mieke de Ridder and Yolandi Potgieter, who are both injured.

This is an important weekend for Zintle Kula and her team. A positive start will be vital. The team will be focused as they will be in action for five consecutive weekends.

The Pro50 match takes place on Saturday and the Pro20 match on Sunday.

Zintle Kula (captain), Gizelle Abrahams, Izel Cilliers, Tiché Frans, Nicolien Janse van Rensburg, Julia Hoal, Palesa Mapoo, Khayakaze Mathe, Nadine Moodley, Anica Swart and Lucian Swartz.

CRICKET NEWS - A hat-trick by Matthew Thorne (5/19) was the highlight in the only SWD Premier League match that was played last weekend.

George was dismissed for a record low score of 90, whereafter Pacaltsdorp only needed 7.3 overs to surpass the target for a nine-wicket victory.

Pacaltsdorp United has now moved to the second position on the log.

George 40 all out (12.5 overs) (Matthew Thorne 5/19, Duren Felix 4/20).

Pacaltsdorp United 44/1 (7.3 overs) (Kurt Botha 21*, Anoxolo Kitshini 19).

title: Boks begin 2027 Rugby World Cup title defence with Italy clash
description: RUGBY NEWS - Current champions South Africa will open their 2027 Rugby World Cup campaign in Australia with a match against Italy at the Adelaide Oval on 3 October, with kick-off at 05:45 (SA

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - Current champions South Africa...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - Current champions South Africa will open their 2027 Rugby World Cup campaign in Australia with a match against Italy at the Adelaide Oval on 3 October, with kick-off at 05:45 (SA time).

The Citizen reports it was announced earlier today when World Rugby released the fixture schedule for the tournament.

Hosts Australia will face Hong Kong China in the opening game on 1 October in Perth, with kick-off at 12:45.

The Boks, who not only won the world cup in France in 2023 but also in Japan in 2019, to go with triumphs in 1995 and 2007, are in Pool B at the world cup.

Their other two matches in pool action at the tournament will be played on Sunday, 10 October, at 08:45 in Brisbane against Georgia and Sunday, 17 October, at 13:15 in Perth against Romania.

For the first time at a Rugby World Cup there will be a round of 16 on the weekend of 23 and 24 October 2027, with the quarter-finals on 30 and 31 October.

The semi-finals will take place on Friday and Saturday, 5 and 6 November, with the final scheduled for 13 November, in Sydney.

The 2027 Rugby World Cup will feature 52 matches, with tickets going on sale on 18 February.

This 11th edition of the World Cup will also, for the first time, comprise 24 teams and be played in seven cities in Australia.

Zimbabwe will play at the World Cup for the first time since 1991, while Hong Kong China will be on debut.

title: Stormers disappointed after letting down 50,000 fans — Dobson
description: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers are bitterly disappointed with what Director of Rugby John Dobson referred to as their worst performance of the season, when they crashed to a 30-19 defeat to the Sharks

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers are bitterly...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers are bitterly disappointed with what Director of Rugby John Dobson referred to as their worst performance of the season, when they crashed to a 30-19 defeat to the Sharks in their United Rugby Championship (URC) coastal derby in Cape Town on Saturday.

The Stormers were heavy favourites heading into the game, after winning all eight of their URC matches until this last weekend, with them top of the log, while the Sharks had never won a URC match in Cape Town before and were languishing down in 14th.

But the Durban side stunned a full house of over 52,000 fans, as they took an early fourth minute lead and never gave it up, as the Stormers fell to their first loss of the competition, and just second loss all season to go with 11 wins.

The defeat has seen the Stormers slip to second on the URC log, three points behind Glasgow Warriors, but they do have a game in hand over the Scottish side, which makes the return clash against the Sharks in Durban this coming weekend all the more important.

“I’ll tell you what feels horrible. The people of Cape Town have come out and we produced comfortably our worst performance of the season and it wasn’t even a thriller at the end,” said a disappointed Dobson after the match.

“From that point of view (the loss) feels terrible. But we’ve got a plan for next week which we’d already made before this result and we’ll be better.”

The Sharks were powered by a brilliant set piece performance, as they dominated the scrums, lineouts and mauled the Stormers off their feet, which are all usually massive areas of strength for the Cape side.

The Stormers were also ill-disciplined, conceding 15 penalties and seeing two players yellow carded, while they also weren’t clinical enough in the Sharks 22m.

“On both sides of the lineout, we were poor. Not converting our 22 metre entries and our discipline also cost us. I don’t mind (conceding penalties), but foul play penalties like tackling a man in the air or whatever we did, those are frustrating,” explained Dobson.

“The silver lining to the dark cloud, I suppose, is that we have the opportunity to travel to Durban to set things right. There is an element that the bogey (of being unbeaten) is off our backs.

“If you looked at the URC stats before this round, we had the top lineout and scrum, and we didn’t put that picture up. However, we will be back next week.”

The Stormers and Sharks will now conclude their coastal derby double header in Durban this coming Saturday and the Cape side will be desperate to bounce back with a win, while the Durbanites will want to continue their resurgence and keep climbing up the URC table.

title: Stormers and Sharks gunning for URC derby glory
description: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers and the Sharks will both be gunning for United Rugby Championship (URC) derby glory when they collide at the Cape Town Stadium on Saturday night (kick-off 19:30). It

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers and the Sharks...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers and the Sharks will both be gunning for United Rugby Championship (URC) derby glory when they collide at the Cape Town Stadium on Saturday night (kick-off 19:30).

It is a match-up between two teams on different ends of the log, with the Stormers styling at the top after winning all eight games so far, while the Sharks are languishing in 14th, having picked up two wins, a draw and five losses over their campaign so far.

Both teams will thus be desperate to win for different reasons, the Stormers to keep their incredible unbeaten run intact, while the Sharks will want to claw their way up the log and back into contention for a place in the top eight.

The Stormers suffered their first defeat of the season, against Harlequins in the Champions Cup just a few weeks ago, but bounced back with a win over Leicester Tigers in Cape Town over the past weekend.

They will be welcoming a few star players back for the big derby, and are expecting to come up against a star studded Sharks team, but in-form flank Paul de Villiers says that although he is excited for a potential battle against Springbok captain Siya Kolisi, they aren’t focusing on who they may face.

“It is any player’s dream to test yourself against the best, whether it be Siya or any of their other (star) players,” admitted De Villiers.

“So I am looking forward to it, but we will focus on our game and not the names the Sharks will bring. They are on the up. It will be important to maintain our standards and every weekend we strive to reach it. Some weekends we do good, others not so.

“We will take confidence into this match (knowing they are unbeaten against the Sharks in Cape Town), but we also know prior results don’t really count in a derby. It is always 50/50. Whoever they bring, won’t impact our preparation.”

The Sharks had a dismal start to the season, but have been improving in recent times, although their results are still up and down.

After a URC win against the Bulls at the end of December, they fell to a loss against the Lions, before losing in the Champions Cup against Sale Sharks, but then bounced back with a big win over Clermont over the past weekend.

Despite their losing run in Cape Town, interim coach JP Pietersen believes his charges can take that momentum from the weekend’s win into the match and break their URC duck in the Cape, with the benches expected to play a big role in the game.

“It’s a match 23 team. There are no more starts or benches. It’s a collective thing. The previous result shows that we came up short but hopefully we can rewrite that this weekend,” said Pietersen.

“We want to win. The only way you can change the story is to play your game and put the Stormers under pressure. They are a good side so you must respect them. But we will be given opportunities, and we must take them.”

title: Tony Brown says he’s staying with Boks … but keen to coach All Blacks in future
description: RUGBY NEWS - Springbok attack coach Tony Brown has revealed that one of Scott Robertson s final acts as All Blacks head coach was an attempt to lure him back to New Zealand. Brown confirmed the

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - Springbok attack coach Tony...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - Springbok attack coach Tony Brown has revealed that one of Scott Robertson’s final acts as All Blacks head coach was an attempt to lure him back to New Zealand.

Brown confirmed the approach during an interview with Newstalk ZB, describing Robertson’s sudden dismissal as “bizarre” and admitting the timing left him deeply frustrated.

It comes just days after Brown effectively ruled himself out of joining another All Blacks coaching candidate, Jamie Joseph, explaining that he has no exit clause in his current Springbok contract, which runs through to 2027.

title: Groot-Brak mik vir stabiliteit in strawwe SWD-liga
description: RUGBY NUUS - Groot-Brak-rugbyklub sê hy is administratief en struktureel gereed vir die nuwe SWD-klubrugbyseisoen met sy spelersdiepte en beskikbaarheid wat vanjaar n deurslaggewende rol

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NUUS - Groot-Brak-rugbyklub sê...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NUUS - Groot-Brak-rugbyklub sê hy is administratief en struktureel gereed vir die nuwe SWD-klubrugbyseisoen met sy spelersdiepte en beskikbaarheid wat vanjaar 'n deurslaggewende rol in hul veldtog kan speel.

Die klub sal vanjaar weer in die SWD Super A-liga meeding, 'n kompetisie wat al hoe strawwer raak weens die fisieke aard en intense meedinging van wedstryde en die druk wat promosie- en relegasiereëls op klubs plaas.

Johan Lerm, direkteur van afrigting van Groot-Brak, het in 'n onderhoud gesê die afrigtingspan is reeds bevestig en in die oefengroep van 40 spelers is daar baie ervaring en ontluikende talent saamgesnoer.

"Ons gaan doelbewus op kontinuïteit staatmaak. Ek het geleer dat stabiliteit in die afrigtingstruktuur en onder die spelers noodsaaklik is, veral in 'n liga waar klein marges groot verskille maak," het hy gesê.

Wat die spelersgroep betref, beskik Groot-Brak tans oor 'n gesonde kern van ervare spelers, aangevul deur jonger manne uit die omgewing.

Onder die ou hande wat weer beskikbaar is, is die kaptein, Marquin Smart, Winrich Kennedy (stut), Justin 'Biggy' Williams (slot), Wilmar 'Boetie' Groenewald (flank), Byron Groenewald (flank) en Byrol Stuurman (flank), om net enkeles te noem.

Twee oudspelers, wat albei in 2023/2024 vir Groot-Brak uitgedraf het toe die klub die SWD-klubkapioen was, is weer terug. Beide Edgar Syfers (losskakel) en Esra Matroos (senter) is vir ons groot aanwinste en gaan met hul ervaring 'n groot verskil in die span maak.

Twee belowende spelers, wat verlede jaar vir die SWD se o. 21's uitgedraf het, is ook vir Groot-Brak beskikbaar. "Boetie Gervinne (flank/agtsteman) en Gilwin Blom (slot) se teenwoordigheid sal beslis die jonger spelers aanspoor en die ouer manne op hul tone hou,"sê Lerm.

Voorseisoen-voorbereiding is reeds volstoom aan die gang en Groot-Brak het twee oefenwedstryde wat op 7 en 14 Februarie voorlê. Lerm reken dié oefenwedstryde sal waardevolle insig gee oor die spelers se fiksheid en strukture.

Hy sê sy grootste uitdaging is om die spelers se kondisionering op te skerp en vir die span om sterk struktureel te speel.

Die klub se primêre doelwit vir die seisoen is om die afrigters en spelers te bemagtig en om die beste uit elkeen te haal.

"Ons het 'n sterk bestuurspan, ervare afrigters, 'n groep entoesiastiese spelers met hope ervaring én belangrik, ons trotse gemeenskapsondersteuners wat altyd bankvas agter die span staan.

"Dis 'n nuwe jaar en nuwe seisoen en ons fokus op die toekoms - wedstryd vir wedstryd. Ek wil ook die spelers aanpor om as rolmodelle vir jeug op te tree, op en van die veld af. Ons wil 'n nalatenskap agterlaat waarop die klub en sy ondersteuners trots kan wees."

title: Bulls highlight discipline ahead of tough away match against Edinburgh
description: RUGBY NEWS - The Bulls have highlighted poor discipline and a need to find balance between running and kicking, before a tough away match to Edinburgh in the United Rugby Championship this weekend.

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - The Bulls have highlighted poor...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - The Bulls have highlighted poor discipline and a need to find balance between running and kicking, before a tough away match to Edinburgh in the United Rugby Championship this weekend.

The Pretoria union come from a tight 26-24 win over Section Paloise (Pau) in their last Champions Cup pool match on the weekend.

The result ended their seven-game losing streak, and though it was their sole win in the pool stage, other results ensured they finished fourth and qualified for a last-16 play-off against Glasgow Warriors in April.

While coach Johan Ackermann warned one win would not turn their whole season around, he said he was grateful his team had shown character to overturn a 21-12 half-time deficit, even though they only finished the game with 34% possession and 38% territory, and Reinhardt Ludwig received a yellow card for repeated infringements.

However, he rued how poor discipline kept them on the back foot. This will not fly against Edinburgh in Scotland, even though the Scottish team are in poor form with two wins from six games, coming from a 63-10 thumping by Bath in the Champions Cup.

“We are still striving for that 80-minute performance,” said the Bulls coach. “Cutting down unforced errors, things like our discipline. We gave away some soft penalties that pinned us down. We had to defend a lot of mauls and 22m entries.”

Ackermann said his team defended well to the last minute, winning a turnover at the death. But they needed to manage the game better for longer.

“No game will be perfect. There will be mistakes and you will be without the ball for certain periods, but I think we can be more clinical with the ball.

“If we want to get more wins and progress in any competition, we have to get the balance right between our kicking and running games.”

He said he could see how the Bulls might have looked like they reverted to their traditional set-piece focused conservative game style. But losing the possession and territory battles, and a rainy second-half, meant they had to kick more than they have been doing recently.

Ackermann pointed to their intercept-try as an example of the more dynamic style they have been aiming for.

Ackermann recently dismissed assistant coaches Andries Bekker and Chris Rossouw, and hired new backline coach Neil de Bruin. Springbok assistant coaches have also been to Bulls training sessions to provide technical input.

title: EPCR wrap: Stormers progress in Champions Cup, Bulls face nervy wait
description: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers secured their place in the Champions Cup playoffs with an unconvincing win over Leicester Tigers in Cape Town, while the Bulls tight win over Section Paloise has given

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers secured their...
page.bodyText:
RUGBY NEWS - The Stormers secured their place in the Champions Cup playoffs with an unconvincing win over Leicester Tigers in Cape Town, while the Bulls tight win over Section Paloise has given them a chance of making it to the knockouts.

The Sharks strong win over ASM Clermont is likely not enough to get them through, and they will probably drop into the Challenge Cup last 16, while the Lions were dumped out of the EPCR after their draw with USA Perpignan in France.

Although it wasn’t the best of performances, the Stormers did their job by downing Leicester 39-26 at the Cape Town Stadium on Saturday evening, to earn a bonus point win to seal their spot in the Champions Cup knockouts, after not qualifying for them last season.

But they have to wait for the result of the match between La Rochelle and Harlequins in France on Sunday evening to see if they will host a playoff, with them needing the French team to beat the English side to take second place in the pool.

In the match a very close first half saw both teams score two tries, but the Stormers took a 15-14 lead into the break thanks to a Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu penalty on the hooter.

The second half was then a back-and-forth affair, with Leicester continuing to stay in touch, and when the clock went red the Stormers only led by six points, before replacement scrumhalf Imad Khan scored a try to make the scoreline look more comfortable than it really was.

“It was a tough game, they were incredibly organised. Even though they were missing a few players I thought it was still a really good Leicester team,” said Stormers Director of Rugby John Dobson after the match.

“In the first half I thought we were pretty flat in the back 20 (minutes), and we weren’t putting pressure on them anywhere. We showed much better fight in the second, but it was a stressful game and there was some great rugby all-round.”

The Bulls also face a wait to see if they will make it to the Champions Cup knockouts, after their thrilling 26-24 win over Pau in France on Friday night lifted them up to fourth in their pool.

They can however be overhauled by Scarlets on Sunday evening, but the Welsh side would need to pick up a very unlikely upset win over English powerhouse Northampton Saints on their home turf to do that.

In the match the Bulls, who went into it on a seven game losing run, had to show tremendous character to fight back from a 21-12 halftime deficit, which became 24-12 after 48 minutes, before inching ahead by two points with eight minutes remaining and held on for a big win which will hopefully reignite their season.

The Sharks gave themselves a chance of reaching the Champions Cup playoffs thanks to a strong second half showing earned them a 50-12 thrashing of ASM Clermont in Durban on Saturday afternoon.

But Stade Toulousain’s 77-7 drubbing of Sale Sharks moved them up into third in the pool and pushed the Sharks down to fifth, and they need Glasgow Warriors to smash Saracens by more than 48 points on Sunday night to lift them back into the playoffs on points difference.

In the match the Sharks were not at their best in the first half and took a tight 14-12 lead into the break, but absolutely turned it on in the second period, scoring 36 unanswered points to secure a very impressive win in the end.

For the Lions their 20-all draw with Perpignan in France on Saturday night meant they finished the pool stage level on points with the French side, but in fifth place in the group and out of the Challenge Cup due to an inferior points difference.

It is very disappointing for the Lions, as they should have been too strong for a number of teams in the pool, such as Newcastle Red Bulls, Welsh side Dragons, and their French hosts Perpignan, but in the end finished below all of them to be unceremoniously dumped out.

In the match the Perpignan were arguably the better team in the first half, but both sides were locked at 10-all at the break, before the Lions dominated the second half thanks to a firing scrum, but the teams again couldn’t be separated on the scoresheet.

title: Tradisie, toekoms en trots hou Georgies aan die gang
description: GEORGE NUUS - Terwyl die spelers reeds sweet op die oefenveld ter voorbereiding vir die nuwe seisoen, bou George-rugbyklub doelgerig aan iets groter as net wedstryde en punte - n volhoubare toekoms

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: GEORGE NUUS - Terwyl die spelers reeds sweet...
page.bodyText:
Correspondent
                                Hugo Redelinghuys

GEORGE NUUS - Terwyl die spelers reeds sweet op die oefenveld ter voorbereiding vir die nuwe seisoen, bou George-rugbyklub doelgerig aan iets groter as net wedstryde en punte - 'n volhoubare toekoms gegrond op mense, waardes en die gemeenskap.

Volgens Jacques van Reenen, president van George-rugbyklub, is die klub se grootste krag nie net wat op die veld gebeur nie, maar die unieke kultuur wat oor dekades heen gevestig is.

"By Georgies is daar 'n spesiale atmosfeer van liefde en samesyn. Ons is meer as 'n rugbyklub - ons is 'n familie," sê hy.

Die klub se leuse, "Beauty for Ashes," weerspieël hierdie ingesteldheid.

"In die wêreld ontvang mense daagliks as, en baie gee as vir as terug. By Georgies probeer ons iets anders doen - ons gee skoonheid terug vir as," verduidelik Van Reenen.

George-rugbyklub bied 'n veilige en respekvolle omgewing vir spelers van alle ouderdomme, met 'n topgehalte oefenveld, hooggeskoolde mediese personeel, 'n opgegradeerde klubhuis, ervare afrigters en 'n bekwame bestuurspan.

"As leiers verstaan ons dat mense ons grootste bate is. Daarom word borgfondse verantwoordelik bestuur, met die welstand van ons spelers en die klub as prioriteit," voeg Van Reenen by.

Die klub, wat in 1883 gestig is en een van die oudste rugbyklubs in Suid-Afrika is, het deur die jare groot aansien geniet en spelers opgelewer wat op die hoogste vlakke van die spel meegeding het. In vroeër jare was George-rugbyklub bekend as die dorp se hartklop, maar veranderende omstandighede bring nuwe uitdagings.

Met die groei van sterk gemeenskapsklubs in George, asook die ligging van die oefenveld, is die stryd om talent groter as ooit. Daarby het die gaping tussen hoërskoolrugby en klubrugby oor die jare vergroot. "Baie jong manne sukkel om die oorgang te maak en kies uiteindelik om ná skool op te hou rugby speel," sê Van Reenen.

George-rugbyklub het hierdie werklikheid in die oë gekyk en fokus doelbewus op mentorskap en geleidelike blootstelling van jong spelers aan seniorrugby. Die vrugte van dié benadering was reeds sigbaar in die 2025-seisoen, toe die klub 'n rekordgetal o. 21-spelers geregistreer het - jong manne wat nou 'n tasbare bydrae tot die klub se DNA lewer.

Maar nie alle uitdagings speel op die veld af nie. In 2023 is die klub swaar getref toe die George-munisipaliteit advertensieborde by die verkeersirkel verwyder het weens wetgewing rakende advertensies naby nasionale paaie. Die gevolg was 'n verlies van ongeveer 70% van die klub se jaarlikse inkomste, wat George-rugbyklub in 'n ernstige finansiële knyp geplaas het.

Hoewel die munisipaliteit onderneem het om 'n muur by die noordpaviljoen van die A-veld te bou om alternatiewe advertensieruimte te skep, het die finansiële impak diep gesny.

"2025 was uit 'n finansiële oogpunt 'n baie moeilike jaar," erken Van Reenen. "Spelers, afrigters en bestuur moes groot opofferings maak - dikwels tot nadeel van hul eie gesinne en persoonlike finansies."

"Ons gee om vir mekaar en bied onder-steuning waar ons kan om die druk van sosiale en ekonomiese omstandighede te help dra," sê Van Reenen. "Die druk is groot, maar ons hou aan vorentoe beweeg."

Lede van George-rugbyklub staan trots bekend as Georgies - 'n naam wat veerkragtigheid, lojaliteit en gemeenskapsgees simboliseer. "Ons staan sterk te midde van die storms wat in ons geliefde stad woed," sê Van Reenen. "Want ons is Georgies - en ons gee nie maklik moed op nie."

title: Arende bou diepte en vuurkrag
description: GEORGE NUUS - Die SWD Arende het n duidelike sein van voorneme vir die komende seisoen uitgestuur deur hul gekontrakteerde spelersgroep wesenlik met 10 nuwe aanwinste, wat pas by die unie aangesluit

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: GEORGE NUUS - Die SWD Arende het 'n...
page.bodyText:
Correspondent
                                Hugo Redelinghuys

GEORGE NUUS - Die SWD Arende het 'n duidelike sein van voorneme vir die komende seisoen uitgestuur deur hul gekontrakteerde spelersgroep wesenlik met 10 nuwe aanwinste, wat pas by die unie aangesluit het, te versterk.

Volgens Heyneke Meyer, die hoofafrigter, is dié uitbreiding deel van 'n doelgerigte plan om 'n groep van 32 spelers saam te stel wat die fisieke eise en intensiteit van die seisoen kan hanteer.

Hy het aan George Herald gesê onderhandelinge met nog 'n paar topspelers in sleutelposisies is tans aan die gang, en verdere aankondigings kan binnekort verwag word.

"Ons wil veral ons diepte op skrumskakel en slot versterk," het Meyer bevestig. "Dit is posisies waar jy eenvoudig nie kan bekostig om dun gesaai te wees nie."

Die Arende se oefengroep, wat Maandag amptelik begin voorberei het, bestaan uit 'n goed gebalanseerde kern van jong spelers, aangevul deur 'n handvol belowende klubspelers. Volgens Meyer is die energie en ingesteldheid binne die groep reeds merkbaar positief.

"Daar is groot opgewondenheid onder die spelers. Die energie wat hulle inbring is aansteeklik en bemoedigend," het hy gesê.

"Vir my is dit uiters belangrik om van die begin af die regte kultuur en gees in die groep te vestig, en al die tekens is daar dat die spelers oorgehaal is om saam daaraan te bou en dit lewendig te hou."

Meyer het bygevoeg dat klubspelers doelbewus by die groep ingeskakel word om blootstelling te kry en ervaring op hoër vlak op te doen. "Ek wil hulle sporadies betrek en ontwikkel. Ek is werklik tevrede met hoe hulle by die groep aanpas."

Met die amptelike seisoen wat op 7 Maart afskop, erken Meyer dat tyd nie aan die Arende se kant is nie. "Nou begin die werklike werk.

"Die tyd is min om die spelers se kondisionering op te skerp, en ons sal vinnig moet inhaal as ons vanjaar mededingend wil wees."

Die Arende se voorbereiding sluit twee opwarmingswedstryde in. Hulle pak OP op 14 Februarie in 'n wegwedstryd aan, voordat 'n moontlike tuiswedstryd teen Grens op 21 Februarie op Outeniquapark wag.

Met nuwe energie, groter diepte en 'n duidelike fokus op kultuur en mede-dingendheid, lyk dit of die SWD Arende oorgehaal is om hul merk in die komende seisoen te maak.

Ervare agterspeler vir die Toyota Cheetahs. Het deur die Blue Bulls-stelsel gevorder en Suid-Afrikaanse Skole verteenwoordig.

Sterkpunte: Veelsydig in die agterste drie, betroubaar onder die hoë bal, sterk skopspel, goeie spoed en posisionering.

Voormalige Golden Lions Jeug- en Tukkies-haker met uitgebreide Varsitybeker- en Shield-ervaring. 'n Goed afgeronde voorryspeler uit toonaangewende ontwikkelingsisteme.

Sterkpunte: Akkurate lynstaaningooie, fisies in kontak, sterk werktempo en goeie ondersteuningspel.

Voormalige Villagers RFC-stut en produk van die WP se juniorstrukture. 'n Betroubare voorryspeler op klubvlak.

Sterkpunte: Fisiese skrumwerk, goeie werktempo, betroubaar in noue spel.

Sterkpunte: Kragtige skrumwerk, fisiese bal-dra, taai spel in noue kontak.

Slot met ervaring in die Blue Bulls-stelsel en uitstaande klubspeler vir Villager FC (Goue Beker-vlak).

Sterkpunte: Lynstaanteenwoordigheid, fisiek in kontak, sterk werkverrigting.

Voormalige Tuks- en Blue Bulls-losvoorspeler met professionele ervaring by die Houston Saber Cats (MLR).

Sterkpunte: Afbreekwerk, hoë werkver-rigting, fisieke drawerk en intense verdediger.

Griquas-skrumskakel. Voormalige SWD Skole- en Oakdale-produk en oudkaptein van die CUT Ixias.

Sterkpunte: Vinnige diens, akkurate aangee, sterk boksskoppe en goeie spelbeheer.

Blue Bulls-jeugproduk met addisionele rugbyliga-ervaring, wat fisiekheid en direktheid na die middelveld bring.

Sterkpunte: Kragtige baldraer, fisieke verdediging, plofbare kontak, hoë intensiteit.

Voormalige Westelike Provinsie-skolesensasie en jeugverteenwoordiger, verbind aan die Stormers/WP-ontwikkelingstrukture.

Sterkpunte: Ontploffende spoed, sterk afrondingsvermoë, atletiese aanvallende bedreiging.

Varsitybeker-stut vir die CUT Ixias met stewige universiteitsvlakervaring in die voorry.

Sterkpunte: Fisiese skrumwerk, sterk baldraer, hoë werktempo rondom die losgemale.

title: De Kock ton carries Proteas to series victory over West Indies
description: CRICKET NEWS - Quinton de Kock ripped the West Indies bowling attack apart on Thursday night, guiding the Proteas to a seven-wicket victory in Centurion as the hosts took an unassailable 2-0

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: CRICKET NEWS - Quinton de Kock ripped the...
page.bodyText:
CRICKET NEWS - Quinton de Kock ripped the West Indies bowling attack apart on Thursday night, guiding the Proteas to a seven-wicket victory in Centurion as the hosts took an unassailable 2-0 lead in their three-match T20 International series against the Caribbean tourists.

Set a target of 222 runs to win, De Kock took the pressure off the SA team, racing to his half-century off just 21 balls.

He went on to knock a career-best 115 off 49 deliveries in an innings which included 10 sixes. It was the opening batter’s second T20 International ton.

And he was well supported by Ryan Rickelton, with the duo racking up 162 runs for the second wicket before spinner Akeal Hosein eventually had De Kock caught by Brandon King at mid-off.

Rickelton made an unbeaten 77 off 36, carrying the SA team to an impressive win with 15 balls to spare.

Earlier, after being sent in to bat, the West Indies players put up a fight, compiling 221/4.

Top-order batters King and Shimron Hetmyer laid a solid foundation, sharing 126 runs for the second wicket.

The stand was broken in the 12th over when King (49 off 30) chipped a delivery from fast bowler Kagiso Rabada to Tristan Stubbs running in from long-on.

Hetmyer went on to bash 75 off 42 but he was dismissed a few overs later when he sliced a ball from Keshav Maharaj to Dewald Brevis at deep point.

Middle-order batter Sherfane Rutherford then contributed a valuable 57 not out off 24 to give his team a defendable total, though it was ultimately not enough to hold off a destructive Proteas side.

Maharaj was the best of the home team’s bowlers, taking 2/22, while Rabada and Jansen grabbed one scalp each.

Speedster Anrich Nortje had a rough day on the park, giving away 59 runs from three overs.

The third and final match of the T20 series, a dead rubber, will be played at the Wanderers on Saturday (18:00 start).

title: Crucial weekend for Garden Route Badgers
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers will have to record an outright win this weekend for a place in the final of the four-day Cricket SA division 2 competition. They will take on the

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers...
page.bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - The Garden Route Badgers will have to record an outright win this weekend for a place in the final of the four-day Cricket SA division 2 competition.

They will take on the Eastern Storm at home in their final pool match of the competition starting today, Thursday 29 January.

The Badgers had a perfect start in this competition, winning their first three games by big margins, but lost badly in their last two away games against the Northern Cape Heat and Knights.

After five matches, the Knights are the log leaders at 103.74 points, followed by Eastern Storm at 90.18. The Badgers are at 86.90 points, and basically need to win outright to advance to the final. The place at the top of the table seems far away - figuratively and literally, as the Knights will travel to White River where they will take on the Mpumalanga Rhinos.

Bonus points are awarded at the first 100 overs of each team’s first innings as follows:

Batting: one point on reaching 150 and 0.02 points for each run thereafter. Bowling: one point for taking three wickets, two points for five wickets, three points for seven wickets and four points for nine wickets.

Fifteen points are awarded for an outright victory and six for a draw.

The Easterns have a formidable team, and the Badgers will have to be clinical and consistent over the next four days.

Good news for the Badgers, though, is that, with the exception of Jade Richter (ankle injury), all players are fit and ready for the important fixture.

Yaseen Valli (captain), Ruan Terblanche, Kyle Jacobs, George van Heerden, Nathan Engelbrecht, Pheko Moletsane, Liam Alder, Banele Cele, Zack Momberg, Sintu Majiza, Benni Hansen, Mondli Khumalo, Heath Richards, Tyrece Karelse and Merrick Brett.

title: Weekend of upsets and high scores in SWD Premier League
description: CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past weekend s SWD Premier League action were two major upsets and three centuries. The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park (301/8) recorded their first victory

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past...
page.bodyText:
CRICKET NEWS - The main features of the past weekend’s SWD Premier League action were two major upsets and three centuries.

The bottom-of-the-log New Dawn Park (301/8) recorded their first victory of the season when they surprised the defending champions, Union Stars I (286 all out), with a 15-run victory in a high-scoring contest.

For New Dawn Park, Leegan Moos (121; 92 balls, 5x6, 15x4) excelled with a century.

The Garden Route Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double-century 232 (114 balls, 23x6, 12x4) as Pacaltsdorp posted a record 420/9 against Union Stars II at the Bridgton Sportsgrounds.

Union Stars II were dismissed for 180 as Pacaltsdorp strengthened its grip on the competition with a 240-run victory.

The Harlequins Forces (110/7) spoiled the weekend for the Knysna Cavaliers (106 all out) with a three-wicket victory in a low-scoring match at the Military Sports Club in Oudtshoorn. For Harlequins, Clayton Manewille excelled with figures of 6/22.

At D’Almeida, thanks to an unbeaten century by the Garden Route Badgers’ wicketkeeper, Nathan Engelbrecht (100*; 121 balls, 4x6, 3x4), Sussex (249/9) remains at the top of the log with a 183-run victory against George (66 all out). Spin bowler Jesse Lewis (5/37) was the bowling hero for Sussex.

New Dawn Park 301/8 (Leegan Moos 121, Tyrece Karelse 43, Danvor Muller 31, Grant Esau 2/61).

Union Stars I 286 (Keano Coerecius 55, Jean Heunis 54, Reagon Rhode 44, Grant Esau 34, Corné Kennedy 30, Dimitri Carelse 4/19, Tyrece Karelse 2/78, Brandon Scheepers 2/55).

Knysna Cavaliers 106 (Mesuli Vuba 37, Clayton Manewille 6/22, Givaldhino Pietersen 2/15, Rhupino Plaatjies 3/35).

Harlequins Forces 110/7 (Camron Avontuur 22, Mondli Khumalo 3/32, Stefan May 2/6).

The Badgers’ batting hero, George van Heerden, scored a double century.

Pacaltsdorp United 420/9 (George van Heerden 232, Pieter Stuurman 42, Leroi Bredenkamp 41, Ineal Arries 3/81, Jayver Philander 2/120).

Union Stars II 180 (André Isaacs 40, Adriaan van der Merwe 38, Jayver Philander 26, Ricardo Majolla 4/36, Anaxolo Khitshini 3/20, Liyabona Malife 2/54).

Sussex 249/9 (Nathan Engelbrecht 100*, Heath Richards 50, Envor Windwaai 2/38).

George 66 (Benni Hansen 28, Jesse Lewis 5/37, Ravin-Lee Kemmies 3/12).

CRICKET NEWS  - Pirates United remained on the top of the table of the SWD Cricket promotion league after a 153-run bonus point victory against Ramblers on Saturday 24 January.

There were also victories for Ladismith (beat Heidelberg United by eight wickets) and George B (beat Riversdale by one wicket).

In the Women’s Division 1 Pacaltsdorp United defeated George by six wickets and Sussex won by 10 wickets against Knysna Cavaliers.

title: Simunye thrashes Paris United in regionals
description: SOCCER NEWS - Lawaaikamp s Simunye FC delivered a commanding performance on Sunday 1 February, defeating Paris United 4-0 in a Safa Eden Regional Men s League fixture at the Lawaaikamp Stadium

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SOCCER NEWS - Lawaaikamp’s Simunye FC...
page.bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - Lawaaikamp’s Simunye FC delivered a commanding performance on Sunday 1 February, defeating Paris United 4-0 in a Safa Eden Regional Men’s League fixture at the Lawaaikamp Stadium in George.

The home side dominated from the outset, with lanky forward Yamkela Ntlaba - a former Imizamo Yethu High School player - leading the charge.

He opened the scoring after just five minutes, slotting home to make it 1-0. Paris United defended resiliently early on, but struggled to create clear chances - one effort even sailed right over the bar.

Simunye doubled their lead midway through the first half when Ntlaba beat Paris goalkeeper Abongile Booysen for his second goal.

Paris pushed forward, and winger Liyema Tukwayo looked dangerous on the left flank, but he spurned a golden one-on-one opportunity, firing straight at Simunye keeper Thatho Maboea.

Moments before half-time, Nandile Jacobs capitalised on a counter-attack to make it 3-0, piling pressure on the visitors. The first half ended with Simunye firmly in control.

In the second half, both teams attacked more openly down the flanks, but Paris United failed to find a breakthrough. Simunye continued to play with confidence and Ntlaba completed his hat-trick to seal a 4-0 victory and secure maximum points.

Both teams remain anchored at the bottom of the log, battling to climb into the top eight.

Speaking after the match, hat-trick hero Ntlaba expressed his delight at performing in front of home supporters.

“It feels good to be back here,” he said. “Scoring against Paris means a lot, and I believe we can turn things around as a team. We’re at the bottom now, but we’ll fight to improve our position on the table.”

title: Orlando Pirates coach Ouaddou reacts to new signings’ debuts
description: FOOTBALL NEWS - Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has heaped praise on new recruits Andre De Jong and Daniel Msendami following their debuts in the Buccaneers 2-0 victory over Magesi FC

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: FOOTBALL NEWS - Orlando Pirates coach...
page.bodyText:
FOOTBALL NEWS - Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has heaped praise on new recruits Andre De Jong and Daniel Msendami following their debuts in the Buccaneers’ 2-0 victory over Magesi FC this past weekend, reports The Citizen.

Ouaddou handed a first outing to both De Jong and Msendami, who joined the Buccaneers from Stellenbosch FC and Marumo Gallants respectively during the January transfer period. Mpho Chabatsane, who was also signed by Pirates from Gallants, was an unused substitute at the Orlando Amstel Arena on Saturday.

De Jong and Msendami made their debuts coming on for Mbuthuma and Moremi respectively in the second half.

“It was part of the plan to give them game time,” said Ouaddou during the post-match conference in Soweto.

“It was the right moment to show them to the fans as well, and to give them the opportunity to start in our stadium. They did well. It’s not easy to come here in a new team, they have to integrate quickly, and I think they did well. The time that I gave them, they used it well. And for me, it’s a lot of hope for us, for the future,” he said.

Commenting on the game, Ouaddou said he was delighted with the result, but was not pleased with his team’s finishing in Orlando.

“We really wanted to win the game, especially for the first game of the season for our fans,” said the Moroccan.

“In terms of the quality of the football that we have played, I think it was really interesting. The phases from the building to progression and to finishing were all fantastic. I think I have to really give the players credit for that. One thing that I appreciate a lot in our game is the way that our players are able to counter press with energy,” he said.

“If I have to be a little bit tough, maybe we could have been more efficient in the last third with maybe two or three more goals at least,” he added.

Meanwhile, Ouaddou has explained the absence of midfielder Sipho Mbule and striker Evidence Makgopa from his match-day squad on Saturday.

“It’s simply a tactical choice, regarding the profile of the opponent.

“We have a lot of games coming, especially next week. Since we are going to play every three days, it’s very important to manage the workload of the players,” explained Ouaddou.

title: George teams drop in regionals
description: SOCCER NEWS - Fast Eleven FC is currently the lone star in the night sky as far as George s teams in the regional league are concerned. They are challenging the league honours in their second

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SOCCER NEWS - Fast Eleven FC is currently...
page.bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - Fast Eleven FC is currently the lone star in the night sky as far as George's teams in the regional league are concerned.

They are challenging the league honours in their second season in elite football, boasting 13 points.

The other George teams in the regional league are, however, battling at this stage of the season.

After a poor start, the Lawaaikamp side Simunye FC is battling to get back their winning formula. They have lost all four of their opening fixtures and are sitting at the bottom of the log.

With only three points, Paris United is hanging on precariously after their fixture against Simunye was postponed last weekend.

The Nelson Mandela University team, Madibaz, is down in 15th position with five points after seven league games. The students lost both their games on the road - going down 2-0 against La liga and losing 2-1 against the Strikers.

Another Lawaaikamp team, the Black Cats FC, descended to seventh spot after last weekend's results away from home - after a number of their experienced players were replaced by U21 players.

title: Fast Eleven lead in regional league
description: SOCCER NEWS - The top-of-the-log Fast Eleven had mixed results in their Safa Eden Regional League fixtures last weekend. On Saturday 24 January, they defeated Smuts United 6-4 at the Smutsville

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SOCCER NEWS - The top-of-the-log Fast Eleven...
page.bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - The top-of-the-log Fast Eleven had mixed results in their Safa Eden Regional League fixtures last weekend.

On Saturday 24 January, they defeated Smuts United 6-4 at the Smutsville sports field in Sedgefield, but went down 3-0 against the spirited FC Rebels at Hornlee Stadium in Knysna in their second game.

With 18 points, Fast Eleven are currently occupying the top spot on the log table. They have played eight games with six victories, lost in two outings, scored 20 goals and conceded 15 goals.

The Fast Eleven skipper, Sihle Mafukula, thanked their coaches, Odwa Gayi and Nkosivile Jubeju, for leading them on their victorious path this season.

He said they have learnt from their mistakes in their less successful first season in this league.

Thanks to a good relationship and understanding between the players and management, the team is at the summit and stands a good chance of putting George on the map again.

title: Lawaaikamp's Black Cats drop vital points at home
description: SOCCER NEWS - The former Safa Eden Regional Men s League champions, Black Cats FC, dropped some important points when they played a 2-2 draw with the newly promoted Flying Eagles from Mossel

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SOCCER NEWS - The former Safa Eden Regional...
page.bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - The former Safa Eden Regional Men's League champions, Black Cats FC, dropped some important points when they played a 2-2 draw with the newly promoted Flying Eagles from Mossel Bay.

Kawaya Henristot and Lithitha Toto scored the two goals for the Cats, while Liyabukwa Dyonta and Sikho Gxikigxa did the same for the Eagles.

Sivuyile Bless (18), a Grade 11 learner of George Royal Academy, stood out as the defender and attacker at the back and was influential on the left side, delivering perfect crosses inside the box.

Henristot scored the first goal for the Black Cats with a penalty kick, which was countered by the Flying Eagles' Liyabukwa 'Mashesha' Dyonta, who levelled the score on 1-1.

In an on-fire second half, Sivuyile Bless of the Black Cats sent a clever cross inside the box, but Mphumzi Kalolo missed the target. A late goal from the Flying Eagles put them in front momentarily, but Lithitha Toto of the Black Cats scored an equaliser with a powerful header.

The draw saw the Black Cats collecting only one point in their two opening games at home.

title: Southern Academy needs funding for Cape Town game
description: SOCCER NEWS - The Thembalethu-based Southern Academy U19 soccer team has qualified for the next round of the prestigious annual Bayhill Premier Cup tournament play-offs after beating MA Colville

page.title: Weather 24°C
page.ogDescription: SOCCER NEWS - The Thembalethu-based Southern...
page.bodyText:
SOCCER NEWS - The Thembalethu-based Southern Academy U19 soccer team has qualified for the next round of the prestigious annual Bayhill Premier Cup tournament play-offs after beating MA Colville 1-0 in a tough game at the Greens Manenberg Sports Group in Cape Town on Saturday 17 January.

John Komichi of Southern Academy sent his side to the next round with his goal.

The team said it is grateful for the opportunity to give young players this platform to showcase their talent.

Southern Academy is looking for financial support for this next trip to Cape Town. Most of the players are still in school, and some adult players are unemployed.

They ask George's communities, stakeholders and church leaders to support them through funding.

Contact one of the team managers, Nkosikhona Maseti, on 076 535 6548.

page.ogDescription: FOOTBALL NEWS - A young soccer player and...
page.bodyText:
FOOTBALL NEWS - A young soccer player and author of three children’s books, Bokang Phiri, has received a life-changing opportunity to play abroad.

According to Roodepoort Record, the 15-year-old from Roodekrans, Roodepoort, has been awarded a 50% soccer and college scholarship at Plymouth College in England, starting this month.

He will be playing and training at the Plymouth Argyle International Academy and said the opportunity is a dream come true and an experience worth its weight in gold.

Bokang started playing soccer at the age of four and it has remained his biggest dream to compete at an international level.

Outside of soccer, he has dedicated himself to promoting a culture of reading among children, particularly in underprivileged communities.

This inspired him to write three children’s books aimed at encouraging children to read for understanding.

“I am extremely grateful and humbled to receive the 50% scholarship. I see it as a reward for years of hard work, discipline and sacrifice.

“The scholarship also eases the financial burden on my family and allows me to focus fully on developing football skills while pursuing my education. Being in a professional environment with quality coaching and exposure will play a major role in shaping my football career and opening future opportunities,” he shares.

Although he has already left South Africa, Bokang still requires financial assistance. His family has launched a BackaBuddy campaign to help cover ongoing costs such as remaining school fees, boarding, training, academic materials and living expenses.

According to his father, Thato, continued community support, no matter how small, will make a significant difference as Bokang settles into this new chapter.
